title: Urban and Regional Planners
description: Develop comprehensive plans and programs for use of land and physical facilities of jurisdictions, such as towns, cities, counties, and metropolitan areas.

tasks.text: Design, promote, or administer government plans or policies affecting land use, zoning, public utilities, community facilities, housing, or transportation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Advise planning officials on project feasibility, cost-effectiveness, regulatory conformance, or possible alternatives.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Create, prepare, or requisition graphic or narrative reports on land use data, including land area maps overlaid with geographic variables, such as population density.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Hold public meetings with government officials, social scientists, lawyers, developers, the public, or special interest groups to formulate, develop, or address issues regarding land use or community plans.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Mediate community disputes or assist in developing alternative plans or recommendations for programs or projects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Recommend approval, denial, or conditional approval of proposals.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct field investigations, surveys, impact studies, or other research to compile and analyze data on economic, social, regulatory, or physical factors affecting land use.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Evaluate proposals for infrastructure projects or other development for environmental impact or sustainability.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Discuss with planning officials the purpose of land use projects, such as transportation, conservation, residential, commercial, industrial, or community use.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Keep informed about economic or legal issues involved in zoning codes, building codes, or environmental regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assess the feasibility of land use proposals and identify necessary changes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Determine the effects of regulatory limitations on land use projects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review and evaluate environmental impact reports pertaining to private or public planning projects or programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise or coordinate the work of urban planning technicians or technologists.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop plans for public or alternative transportation systems for urban or regional locations to reduce carbon output associated with transportation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Identify opportunities or develop plans for sustainability projects or programs to improve energy efficiency, minimize pollution or waste, or restore natural systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Coordinate work with economic consultants or architects during the formulation of plans or the design of large pieces of infrastructure.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Advocate sustainability to community groups, government agencies, the general public, or special interest groups.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Investigate property availability for purposes of development.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct interviews, surveys and site inspections concerning factors that affect land usage, such as zoning, traffic flow and housing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Prepare reports, using statistics, charts, and graphs, to illustrate planning studies in areas such as population, land use, or zoning.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare, develop and maintain maps and databases.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare, maintain and update files and records, including land use data and statistics.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Research, compile, analyze and organize information from maps, reports, investigations, and books for use in reports and special projects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Respond to public inquiries and complaints.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
